simplesect.para: This function is based on FreeBSD implementation but it does not support environment variable: POSIXLY_CORRECT.

parameterdescription.para: String containing the legitimate option characters.

simplesect.para: If an option was successfully found, function returns the option character.

memberdef.argsstring: (int nargc, char *const *nargv, const char *options, const struct sys_getopt_option *long_options, int *idx)
memberdef.name: sys_getopt_long

detaileddescription.para: The sys_getopt_long() function works like sys_getopt() except it also accepts long options, started with two dashes.

simplesect.para: This function is based on FreeBSD implementation but it does not support environment variable: POSIXLY_CORRECT.

parameterdescription.para: String containing the legitimate option characters.
Pointer to the first element of an array of struct sys_getopt_option.
If idx is not NULL, it points to a variable which is set to the index of the long option relative to long_options.

simplesect.para: If an option was successfully found, function returns the option character.

memberdef.argsstring: (int nargc, char *const *nargv, const char *options, const struct sys_getopt_option *long_options, int *idx)
memberdef.name: sys_getopt_long_only

detaileddescription.para: The sys_getopt_long_only() function works like sys_getopt_long(), but '-' as well as "--" can indicate a long option. If an option that starts with '-' (not "--") doesn't match a long option, but does match a short option, it is parsed as a short option instead.

simplesect.para: This function is based on FreeBSD implementation but it does not support environment variable: POSIXLY_CORRECT.

parameterdescription.para: String containing the legitimate option characters.
Pointer to the first element of an array of struct sys_getopt_option.
If idx is not NULL, it points to a variable which is set to the index of the long option relative to long_options.

simplesect.para: If an option was successfully found, function returns the option character.
